headline: Regeneron to Report First Quarter 2026 Financial and Operating Results and Host Conference Call and Webcast on April 29, 2026
summary: TARRYTOWN, N.Y., March 25, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: REGN) today announced that it will report its first quarter 2026 financial and operating results on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, before the U.S. financial markets open. The Company will host a conference call and simultaneous webcast at 8:30 AM Eastern Time that day. Conference Call Information Participants may access the conference call live via webcast on the ’Investors and Media’ page of Regeneron’s web

headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Regeneron (REGN) Securing Japan’s First Targeted Bullous Pemphigoid Therapy Approval
summary: Sanofi and Regeneron announced that Japan’s Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare has approved Dupixent (dupilumab) for adults with moderate-to-severe bullous pemphigoid, based on LIBERTY-BP-ADEPT data showing more than four times as many patients achieved sustained disease remission versus placebo through Week 36. This decision makes Dupixent the first targeted treatment for bullous pemphigoid in Japan and adds a seventh approved indication for the drug in that market, further broadening...

headline: REGN Soars 28.9% in Six Months: Is There More Upside Potential in 2026?
summary: Regeneron's rally reflects biotech momentum as pipeline wins and Dupixent growth offset Eylea erosion amid intensifying competition.

headline: Wells Fargo Raises its Price Target on Amgen (AMGN) to $390 from $375
summary: Amgen Inc. (NASDAQ:AMGN) is one of the 10 Most Profitable S&P 500 Stocks to Buy Now. On March 19, 2026, Wells Fargo raised the price target on Amgen Inc. (NASDAQ:AMGN) to $390 from $375 and maintained an Equal Weight rating. The firm highlighted emerging therapies such as CD20, BAFF/APRIL, and next-generation complement inhibitors as drivers […]
